title: Expert session on Use & Application of critical care equipment (Mechanical Ventilator)
content: Event Date: – 21-01-2025 Event brief description – The expert talk programme was organized by Galgotias school of Nursing under the guidance of Dr. Lekha Bist (Dean SON) .The expert talk on ventilator was held on 21-01-2025, at 11.00am – 12.30pm, bringing together healthcare professionals nursing students, explore advancements and challenges in ventilator design and usage, the event aimed to foster knowledge sharing and collaboration among participants. Ventilators have become a critical component of modern healthcare, especially in light of the COVID-19 pandemic. The session featured a distinguished speaker, Tarun Saini Nursing Officer in Lok Nayak Hospital, Delhi Govt, who shared insights into the evolution, functionality, and future prospects of ventilator technology. Event Detailed Description- The session covered the following topics: Future Directions: Exploring the potential for smart ventilators and advancements in non-invasive ventilation techniques. The expert began by emphasizing the significance of ventilators in managing respiratory failure, particularly in intensive care units. The speaker outlined the primary types of ventilators—invasive and non-invasive—and their applications in different medical scenarios. Attendees were guided through the operational workflow, including: Conclusion and Future Recommendations The expert talk on ventilator technology was an enlightening experience that underscored the vital role of innovation in respiratory care. By delving into both technical and practical aspects, the event equipped participants with a deeper understanding of the challenges and opportunities in this field. Participants are actively engaged asked questions, creating an interactive learning environment. Department Name: Galgotias School of Nursing Event Outcome - Event was a great success which shows a lot of hard work and strength of group who worked altogether to achieve goal.
